Mnemonic: MAA saw the DOG Rising to bark.
I'm in a Low mood in my ROOM (Deuum), so I need to DRINK.
Your jaw Falls when you PHUUT (Speak) too much.
The ROOHNG (Room) for RIIAN (Learning) is a Mid-sized SCHOOL.
In the HAAWNG (Room) for NAAWN (Napping), my head Falls for a Mid-night sleep.
In the HAAWNG (Room) for KHRUUA (Cooking), temperatures Fall while the soup stays Mid-range hot.
BPRA (Press) the DTUU (Do) knob Low to open the DOOR to the Mid-dle room.
My NAA (Face) Falls when I look out the Low WINDOW (Dtaang).
BPIT (Bit) by bit, I lowered the Low window to CLOSE it.
KHIIAN (Keen) students have Rising grades because they WRITE a lot.

Pruet-sa-phaa sounds like "fruit supper". May is the month when we start eating fruit supper outside.

Front: มิถุนายน (มิ.ย.)

Back:
July

Ga-rá-gà-daa sounds like crocodile (or a word with a similar rhythm). July is hot, so imagine the crocodile is relaxing in the heat.

November

Prút-sà-jì-gā sounds like "brutally chill". November is the month when it starts to get brutally chill.
